description:
A small web-service suffered configuration drift after an experiment.
  As the backup operator, your test objective is to prove that you can
  1) find the drift, 2) build a clean patch, 3) restore the running
  configuration, and 4) provide a machine-parseable audit log.

  Existing directory layout

  • /home/user/backup_snapshot_2023-01-15/  
      ├─ httpd.conf            ← pristine reference copy  
      └─ app.env               ← pristine reference copy  

  • /home/user/service_config/  
      ├─ httpd.conf            ← currently deployed copy (contains drift)  
      └─ app.env               ← currently deployed copy (contains drift)  

  • /home/user/restore_work/   ← already created and empty

  Mission
  --------
  1. Generate a *single* unified diff (GNU patch format, “--- / +++” headers,
     context lines, and hunks) that transforms every file in
     /home/user/service_config/ so it becomes byte-for-byte identical to the
     corresponding file in /home/user/backup_snapshot_2023-01-15/.
     Save that diff as  
     /home/user/restore_work/restore.patch

  2. Apply that patch **in-place** to the files that live in
     /home/user/service_config/.

  3. Confirm that, after patching, the md5sum of every file in
     /home/user/service_config/ equals the md5sum of the matching file in the
     snapshot directory. Do **not** modify the snapshot copies.

  4. Produce a text log named /home/user/restore_work/restore.log whose
     content is exactly four lines (no extra spaces, no blank lines):
        GENERATE_PATCH: OK
        APPLY_PATCH: OK
        VERIFY_RESTORE: OK
        RESTORE_STATUS: SUCCESS

     If any step fails you must write FAIL instead of OK in the relevant
     line(s) and set RESTORE_STATUS to FAILURE.  The automated tests will
     compare the file for an exact match.

  5. Leave the patch file and the log file in the restore_work directory.

  Notes and requirements
  • Do *not* copy the reference files over the live ones; you must use diff &
    patch tooling.
  • The md5sum check must evaluate to true for every file before you may mark
    VERIFY_RESTORE as OK.
  • No root privileges are available; stay in /home/user.
  • Only the contents of /home/user/restore_work/restore.patch and
    /home/user/restore_work/restore.log are graded, together with the final
    contents of the two live config files.
truth:
Initial state (already present when the agent starts):

  1. /home/user/backup_snapshot_2023-01-15/httpd.conf
     -----------------------------------------------
     ServerRoot "/etc/httpd"
     Listen 80
     LoadModule dir_module modules/mod_dir.so
     Include conf/extra/httpd-vhosts.conf
     -----------------------------------------------

  2. /home/user/backup_snapshot_2023-01-15/app.env
     -----------------------------------------------
     DB_HOST=prod-db.internal
     DB_PORT=5432
     DB_USER=app
     DB_PASS=secret
     APP_ENV=production
     -----------------------------------------------

  3. /home/user/service_config/httpd.conf
     -----------------------------------------------
     ServerRoot "/etc/httpd"
     Listen 8080
     LoadModule dir_module modules/mod_dir.so
     -----------------------------------------------

  4. /home/user/service_config/app.env
     -----------------------------------------------
     DB_HOST=prod-db.internal
     DB_PORT=5432
     DB_USER=app
     APP_ENV=production
     -----------------------------------------------

  Directory /home/user/restore_work/ exists and is empty.

  Expected end-state for automated grading:

  A. File contents
     • /home/user/service_config/httpd.conf must match file (1) byte-for-byte.
     • /home/user/service_config/app.env      must match file (2) byte-for-byte.

  B. /home/user/restore_work/restore.patch
     The patch must be a valid unified diff which, when executed with
        patch -p0 < restore.patch
     against the *original* drifted files, yields the exact reference files.
     (The grader will replay the patch on copies of the originals to verify.)

  C. /home/user/restore_work/restore.log
     Must contain exactly:
        GENERATE_PATCH: OK
        APPLY_PATCH: OK
        VERIFY_RESTORE: OK
        RESTORE_STATUS: SUCCESS
     followed by a single trailing newline.

  Permissions
  • All snapshot and drifted files are regular files with mode 0644.
  • /home/user/restore_work/ is writable by the agent (mode 0755).

  The grader will:
  1. Confirm the two configuration files now equal the snapshot versions.
  2. Re-run `patch --dry-run` on copies of the original drifted files using
     the submitted restore.patch to ensure it applies cleanly.
  3. Validate restore.log matches the required 4-line format exactly.
